instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Why Is There A Surge in Term Insurance Premiums?
Meta description: Of late, term insurance premiums have seen multiple hikes owing to the COVID-19 pandemic. However, you don’t have to worry about the surge impacting your finances.
Keywords: term insurance premium increase, term plan premium increase, term insurance policy, term insurance premium
Thanks to low premiums, a term insurance policy has always been the most affordable life insurance policy in India. Since it is a pure protection plan, you can get a large life cover of around Rs. 1 crore or more at very affordable premiums.
However, things are slowly changing, with term insurance premiums getting a little expensive. Although a term plan is still the most affordable life insurance choice, the premiums that you’re required to pay towards it have seen a surge.
Why Might Term Insurance Premiums Face a Hike?
One of the primary reasons, if not the only one, for this sudden and steep term plan premium increase can be attributed to the COVID-19 pandemic. Ever since the pandemic started to wreak havoc across the world, many insurance companies have been facing waves upon waves of insurance claims due to deaths resulting from COVID-19.
Due to the sudden increase in the number of death claims, insurance companies have had no other option but to tighten the underwriting norms. This includes increasing the term insurance premiums to offset the losses that the insurance companies are finding themselves in.
Why You Shouldn't Worry About the Hike
While the term insurance premium increase isn’t something that can be taken lightly, there’s also no need for you to panic and start worrying about the hike. Despite the hike, India continues to have some of the lowest term premiums in the entire world.
That’s not all. A major reason for you to not worry is that there are several ways for you to ensure that the term plan premiums stay affordable and within your reach. Here’s a quick look at some of the ways through which you can keep your premiums more cost effective.
Compare multiple plans before purchasing
The term insurance premium increase isn’t something that’s constant across insurance service providers. While some of them have chosen to increase their premiums by 25% to 30%, others have managed to keep the increase down to around 10% to 15%.
As a matter of fact, there are a couple of insurance providers who have even continued to keep their premiums constant. And so, before you go ahead with your purchase, it is advisable to compare multiple plans with one another. This way, you can easily determine the one that’s the most affordable and choose to go ahead with that. That’s not all. The term insurance premium increase is also dependent on various factors such as the insurance company’s solvency ratio, quality of their books, and their claim ratios as well. So, that’s again something that you should also factor in when comparing multiple plans with one another.
Get a term plan as early as possible
Another way to ensure that your term insurance policy premiums remain affordable despite the hike is by purchasing one as early on in your life as possible. When it comes to life insurance, age is one of the major factors that determine the premium.
Therefore, when you opt for a term plan while you’re young, you not only get the benefit of lower insurance premiums, but can also lock-in those rates for the rest of the tenure as well. Ideally, it is advisable to purchase a term plan with a long tenure as soon as you start working. This way, you can not only ensure your family’s protection early on, but can also enjoy lower premiums for the rest of your tenure.
Don’t overinsure yourself
Many individuals, in a bid to maximise protection, end up overinsuring themselves. Now, although overinsurance may not be as serious as underinsurance, it can have its drawbacks. Some of the few factors that have a direct correlation with term insurance premiums are the life cover, and the number of riders and the rider sum assured that you opt for.
So, when you opt for more number of riders than necessary or choose a life cover that’s more than what you need, you invariably end up causing a term plan premium increase. That’s why it is essential to ensure that you only opt for what you need. This way, you can to a large extent keep your premiums affordable despite the increase.
Conclusion
Also, make sure that you live and maintain a healthy lifestyle. Insurance companies tend to view healthy people as low-risk individuals and can help keep your insurance premiums low.
Purchasing term plans online can also help keep your premiums low considering the fact that online plans are almost always more affordable than offline insurance plans as they don’t come with additional expenses like agent’s commission.
So, there you have it! Although the term insurance premium increase is something that’s unavoidable, following the ways listed above can help lessen the impact considerably.
